title: Warm French Mushroom Fold
description: A garlicky, shaggy brunch-mood French dish featuring mushrooms and a fold, low-carb-friendly. Perfect for a weekend morning.

ingredients.name: Mushrooms
ingredients.notes: Button or cremini mushrooms work well.

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: Salted or unsalted, whichever you prefer.

ingredients.name: Cream
ingredients.notes: Half-and-half or heavy cream work well too.

ingredients.name: Thyme
ingredients.notes: Fresh or dried thyme, whichever you have on hand.

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a small skillet over medium heat.
Add the sliced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes.
Add the mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the thyme, salt, and pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and a pinch of salt until well combined.
In a greased 9-inch skillet or sauté pan, melt the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat.
Pour half of the egg mixture into the skillet and cook until the edges start to set, about 1-2 minutes.
Sprinkle half of the cooked mushrooms over the eggs, followed by half of the Parmesan cheese.
Use a spatula to gently fold the other half of the eggs over the filling.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the eggs are almost set and the cheese is melted.
Use a spatula to carefully slide the warm French mushroom fold onto a plate and serve immediately.
Reheat the remaining mushroom mixture in the skillet and serve on the side, if desired.
Serve warm, garnished with additional thyme and Parmesan cheese if desired.
